deep_dive.title: A Three-Phase Foundation Model for Tax-Aware Personalized Portfolio Management
deep_dive.abstract: We present a three-phase deep reinforcement learning system for personalized portfolio management that addresses three limitations shared by all prior financial RL work: 1) ticker lock-in, 2) monolithic objectives , and 3) static user models. Phase 1 pretrains a ticker-identity-free cross asset encoder via self-supervised learning on a multi-asset corpus, augmented by a frozen parallel branch using Chronos, a T5-based time series foundation model, fused via a learned gating mechanism. To our knowledge, this is the first application of a time series foundation model to portfolio management RL. The encoder generalizes to any publicly traded asset via a 50-dimensional observable metadata vector that requires no retraining for new tickers. Phase 2 fine-tunes a MoE (Mixture of Experts) portfolio actor critic with PPO under an objective-conditioned reward that simultaneously serves six distinct investment goals sampled per episode: short-term alpha, short-term gain, long-term gain, capital preservation, tax-loss harvesting, and long-term-gains-only. A MoE architecture assigns each objective to a specialized expert head (momentum, growth, defensive, tax-aware), and a learned intent router blends experts based on the active objective and current market regime, which eliminates cross-objective gradient conflict. Phase 3 adds a lightweight personalization layer further adapted at inference time to each individual via a 76-parameter LoRA module fine-tuned on real brokerage transaction history, inferring investment objectives from revealed trading behavior rather than questionnaires. A natural language intent parser converts free-form goals directly into structured investment objective parameters.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: three-phase deep reinforcement learning system for personalized portfolio management, addressing ticker lock-in and static user models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 三阶段深度强化学习系统用于个性化投资组合管理，解决代码锁定和静态用户模型问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 三阶段深度强化学习系统用于个性化投资组合管理，解决代码锁定和静态用户模型问题

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a three-phase DRL system for tax-aware portfolio management: pretraining a ticker-identity-free encoder with a time series foundation model, multi-objective RL, and personalization, enabling generalization to new assets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对金融RL中存在的代码锁定、单一目标和静态用户模型三大局限，本文提出三阶段深度强化学习系统用于税务感知的个性化投资组合管理。第一阶段利用自监督学习预训练跨资产编码器并融合时间序列基础模型，第二阶段进行多目标RL训练，第三阶段实现个性化。该方法首次将时间序列基础模型应用于投资组合RL，使编码器可泛化到新资产，无需重新训练，提升了方法的适应性和实用性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对金融RL中存在的代码锁定、单一目标和静态用户模型三大局限，本文提出三阶段深度强化学习系统用于税务感知的个性化投资组合管理。第一阶段利用自监督学习预训练跨资产编码器并融合时间序列基础模型，第二阶段进行多目标RL训练，第三阶段实现个性化。该方法首次将时间序列基础模型应用于投资组合RL，使编码器可泛化到新资产，无需重新训练，提升了方法的适应性和实用性。

deep_dive.title: Agent Reinforcement Learning via Pivotal-Aware Self-Feedback Retry
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents have shown strong decision-making capabilities in long-horizon interactive tasks, yet they still struggle to effectively leverage failed trajectories: full retries incur high interaction costs, while experience retrieval tends to dilute critical experience signals. To address this, we propose PivoARL, a self-feedback retry framework for experience exploitation in LLM agents. PivoARL identifies the pivotal erroneous turn through structured reflection and performs local retry only from the corresponding pivotal state, thereby reusing the correct prefix and reducing redundant interactions. From an information-gain perspective, we further show that pivotal retry concentrates useful experience signals near the error boundary, mitigating the signal dilution caused by state-agnostic experience utilization. Based on this insight, we design a pivotal-aware credit assignment mechanism that rewards correct prefixes while isolating erroneous suffixes, and optimize reflection quality through implicit reflection returns. We conduct a systematic evaluation on 4 agent tasks and 7 search-based QA benchmarks. Results show that PivoARL achieves significant improvements on Pass@2/3 across all tasks, with an average gain of about 11.5\% over MetaRL. Moreover, benefiting from contrastive preference signals induced by pivotal turns, PivoARL also consistently improves Pass@1 on over 80\% of the tasks. On Minesweeper environment, PivoARL improves over GiGPO by more than 45\% and reduces interaction turns by about 42\% on average compared with full-retry methods. Code is available at https://github.com/yuki-younai/PivoARL.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: agent RL framework leveraging failed trajectories for post-training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用失败轨迹进行智能体强化学习后训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用失败轨迹进行智能体强化学习后训练
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用失败轨迹进行智能体强化学习后训练
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PivoARL, a self-feedback retry framework that improves LLM agents by retrying only from pivotal error turns, making experience exploitation more efficient.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有 LLM 智能体难以有效利用失败轨迹：完全重试成本高，经验检索会稀释关键信号。本文提出 PivoARL，通过结构化反思识别关键错误轮次，仅从关键状态局部重试，复用正确前缀并减少冗余交互；从信息增益角度看，关键重试将有用经验信号集中在错误边界附近。实验表明该方法能提升智能体在长序列交互任务中的决策性能，为智能体后训练提供了更高效的经验利用范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有 LLM 智能体难以有效利用失败轨迹：完全重试成本高，经验检索会稀释关键信号。本文提出 PivoARL，通过结构化反思识别关键错误轮次，仅从关键状态局部重试，复用正确前缀并减少冗余交互；从信息增益角度看，关键重试将有用经验信号集中在错误边界附近。实验表明该方法能提升智能体在长序列交互任务中的决策性能，为智能体后训练提供了更高效的经验利用范式。

deep_dive.title: Spinning Straw into Gold: Relabeling LLM Agent Trajectories in Hindsight for Successful Demonstrations
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model agents operate in partially observable, long-horizon settings where obtaining supervision remains a major bottleneck. We address this by utilizing a source of supervision overlooked in existing post-training methods: unintended yet successful goals embedded within agent rollouts. Specifically, we introduce Hindsight Supervised Learning (HSL), where an auxiliary LLM reviews each completed trajectory and relabels it with all of the natural-language goals the agent actually achieved. HSL then pairs the trajectory with its relabeled goals and uses these pairs for additional fine-tuning. To mitigate suboptimality in the relabeled data, we propose two learning techniques for HSL, irrelevant-action masking and sample reweighting. Our experiments show that HSL is flexible and compatible with existing post-training pipelines. It improves both SFT and DPO, with larger gains on long-horizon tasks with more diverse goal spaces. Moreover, HSL is sample-efficient: on ALFWorld, it surpasses baselines trained on the full dataset while using only one quarter of the ground-truth demonstrations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Introduces hindsight relabeling for LLM agent trajectories to create supervision, a post-training technique for agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 引入对LLM智能体轨迹进行事后重新标记以生成监督信号的后训练方法。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 引入对LLM智能体轨迹进行事后重新标记以生成监督信号的后训练方法。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 引入对LLM智能体轨迹进行事后重新标记以生成监督信号的后训练方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Uses auxiliary LLM to review completed trajectories and relabel them with achieved goals, then fine-tunes on these paired data with techniques like irrelevant-action masking and goal-conditioned regularization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体在部分可观测、长程任务中监督信号稀缺的问题，提出事后监督学习（HSL），通过辅助LLM审查已完成轨迹并重新标记所有已达到的目标，将轨迹-目标对用于额外的微调，并引入无关动作掩码和目标条件正则化来缓解重标记数据中的次优性。实验证明HSL能有效从失败或半成功轨迹中挖掘成功信号，提升智能体在复杂环境中的任务完成率。

deep_dive.title: A Few Teacher Steps Go a Long Way: Cost-Efficient On-Policy Data Augmentation for Agent Post-Training
deep_dive.abstract: For LLM agents, supervised fine-tuning is not only about teacher labels' quality, but also about which interaction contexts those labels condition on. Pure behavioral cloning uses full teacher demonstrations, creating a mismatch between teacher-induced contexts seen in training and student-induced contexts encountered at test time. Recent work addresses this mismatch by querying a teacher at contexts reached by the student, often with increasingly elaborate filtering of the teacher's continuations. We instead frame on-policy data construction as a budget-allocation problem: under matched supervision resources, should teacher output be spent on more start-to-finish demos, longer continuations, outcome filtering, or broader coverage of learner-induced contexts? We formalize this design space through the rollout policy, switch-time distribution, continuation horizon, filtering rules, and two complementary costs: teacher inference generated before filtering and teacher supervision retained for SFT. Across HotpotQA, ALFWorld, and Terminal-Bench-Dev, bounded unfiltered teacher continuations at learner-induced contexts improve over pure behavioral cloning at matched budgets. On HotpotQA and ALFWorld, where we run the full comparison, few-step continuations match or exceed success-filtered and critical-context-filtered alternatives. Our findings suggest that a few teacher steps, placed at learner-induced contexts, can be a more cost-efficient supervision allocation than longer or more heavily curated teacher completions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a cost-efficient on-policy data augmentation method for agent post-training, directly addressing techniques for post-training of autonomous agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出了一种用于智能体后训练的成本高效的在策略数据增强方法，直接针对自主智能体的后训练技术。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出了一种用于智能体后训练的成本高效的在策略数据增强方法，直接针对自主智能体的后训练技术。

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses context mismatch in behavioral cloning for LLM agents by framing on-policy data construction as budget allocation; uses teacher demonstrations at student-reached contexts to improve efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体在行为克隆中面临的上下文不匹配问题，提出一种在策略数据构建的预算分配框架。通过让教师模型在智能体自身产生的上下文上进行少量步骤示范，以最小成本生成高质量训练数据。实验表明该方法在同等资源下优于使用完整演示和过度过滤的方法，为智能体后训练提供了高效数据增强策略。

deep_dive.title: Trading Confidence: Comprehensive Uncertainty Estimation in Algorithmic Trading
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful approach in financial trading, enabling agents to learn optimal strategies through direct market interaction. However, financial markets are highly uncertain, with price fluctuations driven by stochastic volatility, model limitations, and regime shifts. Traditional RL models struggle in dynamic environments, often failing to adapt to sudden market disruptions, leading to suboptimal trading decisions. To address this challenge, we propose an uncertainty-aware RL framework that integrates distributional, epistemic, and aleatoric uncertainty estimations. Our approach enhances uncertainty estimation using SHAP-weighted reconstruction uncertainty, MC Dropout, and an LSTM-based technical indicator consensus mechanism. Experimental results on five major U.S. stock indices demonstrate that RL agents equipped with uncertainty estimation significantly outperform traditional models in return and risk management. This study advances uncertainty estimation in RL-based financial trading, with future research extending its application to other asset classes and alternative RL architectures for greater adaptability.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes an uncertainty-aware RL framework for financial trading agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出面向金融交易智能体的不确定性感知强化学习框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出面向金融交易智能体的不确定性感知强化学习框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出面向金融交易智能体的不确定性感知强化学习框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: An RL framework that integrates multiple uncertainty estimations to improve trading agent robustness in volatile markets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 金融市场的剧烈波动和不确定性给强化学习交易智能体带来挑战。本文提出一种集成分布不确定性、认知不确定性和偶然不确定性的强化学习框架，利用SHAP加权重构不确定性、MC Dropout等技术提升智能体对市场变化的适应能力，在交易回测中表现出更高的稳健性。

quick_skim.title: Aligning Language Models with Selective Prediction
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as critical decision-making components in high-stakes real-world AI systems, rendering LLM reliability a foremost practical concern. In this paper, we focus on enhancing LLM reliability through selective prediction (SP), a strategy that allows an LLM to only predict for inputs where it is likely to be correct (i.e., coverage) and hence reduce the error rate (i.e., risk) on that portion of inputs -- flagging the remaining inputs for future human discretion. In other words, SP improves LLM reliability by balancing the risk-coverage trade-off and enabling seamless human-AI collaboration. To integrate SP into LLMs, we focus on the LLM post-training alignment stage and propose to align LLMs with SP performance metrics, in contrast with existing LLM alignment methods that focus primarily on correctness or calibration metrics. Specifically, we propose a novel alignment framework, Reinforcement Learning for Selection Reward (RLSR), which targets the area under the risk-coverage curve (AURC) -- a popular SP performance metric -- as its alignment objective. RLSR achieves substantially better risk-coverage trade-off compared to multiple alignment baselines on both in-domain and out-of-domain tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 后训练对齐阶段引入选择性预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 后训练对齐阶段引入选择性预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 后训练对齐阶段引入选择性预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes integrating selective prediction into LLM post-training alignment to improve reliability by only predicting on inputs likely correct, balancing risk-coverage tradeoff.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型在高风险实际系统中可靠性的关键问题，本文提出在后训练对齐阶段整合选择性预测（SP），通过平衡风险与覆盖率的权衡，使模型仅对正确的输入预测并标记其余输入供人工裁决。该方法提升了人机协作的流畅性，为构建可靠AI组件提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: A-TMA: Decoupling State-Aware Memory Failures in Long-Term Agent Memory
quick_skim.abstract: Long term memory lets LLM agents act as persistent assistants, but user facts change. A useful memory system must know what is true now, what used to be true, and what changed. We study \emph{ghost memory}, a state coordination failure in which old, current, and transition facts coexist in the memory bank, remain mixed during retrieval, and mislead the answer model. We argue that memory systems should be understood and optimized from three levels: bank maintenance, retrieval, and answer time resolution. We propose ATMA, a state aware overlay for existing memory systems. ATMA keeps superseded and transition records in the bank, builds evidence packets for the query's requested state view, and exposes current, historical, and transition labels to QA. We further call for decoupled evaluation of bank, retrieval, and answer level failures, since final QA accuracy can hide where ghost memory occurs. To make this failure measurable, we build LTP (LoCoMo Temporal Plus), a conflict heavy benchmark for ghost memory, and evaluate on LoCoMo for long conversation generalization. On LTP, Graphiti+ATMA improves conflict accuracy by 0.240 absolute over Graphiti. On LoCoMo, Graphiti+ATMA raises temporal F1 from 0.0295 to 0.1705. The gains are host dependent, but they indicate that explicit state roles can reduce memory failures hidden by final QA accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: state-aware memory overlay for long-term dialogue agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向长期对话智能体的状态感知记忆覆盖层
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向长期对话智能体的状态感知记忆覆盖层
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向长期对话智能体的状态感知记忆覆盖层
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Addresses ghost memory in LLM agents by proposing A-TMA, a state-aware memory overlay that improves factual consistency across state changes, critical for persistent assistants and multi-turn dialogue.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体长期记忆中旧事实与当前事实混淆的ghost memory问题，提出状态感知记忆覆盖层A-TMA，从银行维护、检索和回答时解析三层面优化，保持事实记录的状态一致性。实验表明，A-TMA能有效区分当前、历史与过渡状态事实，提升多轮对话中智能体的记忆准确性和回答可靠性，为构建金融领域多轮对话智能体的记忆系统提供了关键技术。
